item.title: Integration tests for prerequisite detection — test against real Docker, Node, Ollama binaries
item.intent: Prerequisite detection (Docker version, Node version, Ollama availability) is tested with mocked child_process.execFile. If the output format of 'docker --version' changes, or if our version parsing regex is wrong, we won't know until a user hits it.
item.description: src/build/installer/prereqs.test.ts mocks the entire node:child_process module. The mock provides helpers like succeedsWith() and fails() to simulate command output. The real execFile() is never called. This means: (1) version parsing regexes are tested against hardcoded strings, not real binary output, (2) error handling for 'command not found' is simulated, not real, (3) Docker socket permissions, PATH issues, and platform differences are invisible. This item adds integration tests that run real binaries when available and skip gracefully when not.
steps: Test: if Docker is available, run the real version check and verify parsing succeeds
Test: if Node is available (it always is in CI), run the real version check
Test: if Ollama is available, run the real availability check
Skip tests gracefully when binaries are not installed (not failures)
Run on CI where Docker is available (GitHub Actions ubuntu-latest has Docker)
acceptanceCriteria: Integration tests run real prerequisite checks against installed binaries
Version parsing succeeds against real Docker and Node output
Tests skip gracefully on machines where Docker or Ollama is not installed
Existing mock-based unit tests remain unchanged
